nodes.name: Dockerfile
nodes.summary: Multi-stage Docker build for the PharmAgent FastAPI backend: a builder stage installs pinned deps into a venv, and a slim runtime stage copies the venv plus app code, runs as a non-root user, and serves uvicorn on port 8000 with a /api/health healthcheck.
nodes.languageNotes: Multi-stage build isolates pip/build deps from the runtime image; drops privileges to a fixed-uid non-root user and mounts a writable /data volume for the SQLite DB.

nodes.name: builder
nodes.summary: First build stage (python:3.13-slim) that creates a virtualenv at /opt/venv and installs pinned requirements, keeping build tooling out of the final image.

nodes.name: runtime
nodes.summary: Final runtime stage (python:3.13-slim) that copies the prebuilt venv and app/sample_data, sets data-dir env vars, runs as non-root user 'pharma', exposes port 8000, and launches uvicorn for app.main:app.

nodes.name: .dockerignore
nodes.summary: Docker build-context exclusion list that keeps virtualenvs, caches, tests, local data, SQLite DBs, secrets, and git metadata out of the image build context.

nodes.name: .env.example
nodes.summary: Template environment file documenting all PHARMAGENT_* runtime settings (optional Anthropic LLM key/model, SQLite storage paths, bearer-token auth, CORS origins, branding). All vars are optional — the app defaults to keyless MockLLM and open dev mode.
nodes.languageNotes: Every variable is commented out by default; absence of a key triggers the deterministic MockLLM fallback, and an unset API token leaves auth in open dev mode.

nodes.name: requirements.txt
nodes.summary: Pinned production dependency manifest for the FastAPI backend: web stack (fastapi, uvicorn, starlette, pydantic, pydantic-settings), numeric/scientific compute (pandas, numpy, scipy), DOCX export (python-docx), multipart uploads, and the anthropic LLM client.
nodes.languageNotes: Exact-version pins (==) across all runtime packages enforce reproducible builds.

nodes.name: requirements-dev.txt
nodes.summary: Development and test tooling manifest, excluded from the production image: includes runtime deps via -r requirements.txt, plus pytest, httpx (test client), ruff (linter), and pip-audit (dependency vulnerability scanner).

nodes.name: ruff.toml
nodes.summary: Ruff linter configuration for the backend: 120-char line length, py313 target, and a curated high-signal rule set (pyflakes, pycodestyle, import-order, pyupgrade) with stylistic rules (E701/E702/E731/E741) and bugbear deliberately disabled for the compact numeric code style.
nodes.languageNotes: Intentionally excludes flake8-bugbear and compact-numeric-style rules (semicolons, lambda aliases); tests are exempted from line-length (E501).

nodes.name: .understandignore
nodes.summary: Ignore-pattern file scoping the Understand-Anything codebase scan, excluding data, caches, notebooks, and the retired graphify output.

nodes.name: scan-result.json
nodes.summary: Project-scanner inventory artifact for the Understand-Anything pipeline, listing every file with detected language, line count, and file category.
nodes.languageNotes: Generated JSON intermediate consumed by downstream graph-assembly stages; not hand-edited.

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Empty package marker making `app` an importable Python package.

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Empty package marker making `app.agents` an importable Python package.

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Empty package marker making `app.compute` an importable Python package.

nodes.name: base.py
nodes.summary: Defines the Agent base class and AgentResult: an agent owns a system prompt and bound tools, mutating PharmState only through the audited, write-checked tool registry in a bounded tool-use loop.

nodes.name: Agent
nodes.summary: Base agent owning a system prompt and registry-bound tools; runs a bounded tool-use turn that executes tools (enforcing audit and write-access) and never calls other agents.

nodes.name: AgentResult
nodes.summary: Dataclass bundling an agent turn's resulting state, messages, and tool calls.

nodes.name: definitions.py
nodes.summary: Phase-1 roster of domain agent definitions (system prompts and descriptions) used by the supervisor for routing and by the orchestrator to instantiate agents.

nodes.name: supervisor.py
nodes.summary: Level-0 supervisor that routes a user message to a domain agent via weighted keyword scoring, falling back to LLM classification when ambiguous.

nodes.name: Supervisor
nodes.summary: Two-stage router: weighted keyword scoring over domain terms, then LLM classification on ambiguity; bypassed when a workflow template drives routing.

nodes.name: adversarial.py
nodes.summary: Adversarial review engine: a clean-context skeptic that independently and deterministically refutes analysis results, recomputing key NCA/NLME quantities from raw data with method-agnostic arithmetic to catch bugs in the primary code.
nodes.languageNotes: Distrusts tool-written numbers and re-derives Cmax/AUC plausibility independently.

nodes.name: _nca_internal
nodes.summary: Internal NCA consistency checks (e.g. AUCinf below AUClast, non-positive CL/F, extrapolation out of range) emitting severity-graded findings.

nodes.name: _nca_dose_monotonic
nodes.summary: Flags non-monotonic dose-vs-exposure trends across dose groups as a medium-severity finding.

nodes.name: _nlme_refute
nodes.summary: Refutes NLME fits by flagging over-parameterization and non-converged results.

nodes.name: _obs_by_subject
nodes.summary: Groups raw observations by subject from a dataframe using role mappings, for independent recomputation.

nodes.name: _nca_recompute
nodes.summary: Recomputes Cmax and an AUC plausibility band directly from raw observations to detect disagreement with reported NCA values.

nodes.name: review
nodes.summary: Top-level adversarial review: runs internal checks plus raw-data recomputation against a state dump and goal, returning severity-sorted findings.

nodes.name: bioequivalence.py
nodes.summary: Average bioequivalence assessment computing geometric mean ratios and 90% confidence intervals (paired crossover and parallel designs) against the 80-125% acceptance window.

nodes.name: be_one_parameter
nodes.summary: Assesses bioequivalence for a single PK parameter, dispatching to paired or parallel computation.

nodes.name: _be_paired
nodes.summary: Paired (crossover) bioequivalence: log-scale mean difference, standard error, and CI on test/reference exposures.

nodes.name: _be_parallel
nodes.summary: Parallel-design bioequivalence: two-group log-scale comparison with pooled variance and CI.

nodes.name: assess_bioequivalence
nodes.summary: Assesses bioequivalence across all common PK parameters, blocking overall BE if any parameter fails.

nodes.name: compartmental.py
nodes.summary: Closed-form one- and two-compartment oral PK models (single-dose and steady-state) plus least-squares fitting of individual subjects and dose-grouped cohorts.
nodes.languageNotes: Analytic concentration solutions vectorized over time; steady-state via superposition accumulation factor.

nodes.name: conc_1cmt_oral
nodes.summary: Closed-form 1-compartment oral concentration over time given ka, CL, V.

nodes.name: conc_2cmt_oral
nodes.summary: Closed-form 2-compartment oral concentration solving the biexponential disposition from ka, CL, V1, Q, V2.

nodes.name: conc_1cmt_oral_ss
nodes.summary: Steady-state 1-compartment oral concentration over a dosing interval via superposition.

nodes.name: conc_2cmt_oral_ss
nodes.summary: Steady-state 2-compartment oral concentration over a dosing interval via superposition accumulation.

nodes.name: _initial_estimates
nodes.summary: Derives initial parameter guesses (ka, CL, V) from observed time-concentration data before fitting.

nodes.name: fit_one_subject
nodes.summary: Fits a single subject's concentration-time profile to a chosen compartmental model by nonlinear least squares, returning parameters and fit metrics.

nodes.name: fit_compartmental
nodes.summary: Fits compartmental models across subjects grouped by dose, selecting the best model per group.

nodes.name: fit_compartmental_ss
nodes.summary: Fits steady-state compartmental models to extracted steady-state interval profiles.

nodes.name: diagnostics.py
nodes.summary: Pure, deterministic model goodness-of-fit residual diagnostics (log-scale weighted residuals and NPDE) built on the structural PK/PK-PD simulator.
nodes.languageNotes: Log-scale residuals for proportional error; NPDE via simulation under IIV with fixed seed for determinism.

nodes.name: fit_residuals
nodes.summary: Computes population and individual weighted residuals (log scale) by pairing observations with typical and individual predictions, returning summary statistics.

nodes.name: npde
nodes.summary: Computes normalized prediction distribution errors by simulating each observation under the population IIV model with a fixed seed.

nodes.name: dose_proportionality.py
nodes.summary: Power-model dose-proportionality assessment: fits log(exposure) vs log(dose), computing the slope, confidence interval, and a critical-region acceptance test.

nodes.name: power_model
nodes.summary: Fits the power model of exposure vs dose, returning slope, standard error, CI, and the proportionality critical-region decision.

nodes.name: assess_dose_proportionality
nodes.summary: Aggregates power-model dose-proportionality assessments across all PK parameters.

nodes.name: dose_sweep.py
nodes.summary: Dose-sweep simulator computing steady-state exposure metrics (AUC, Cmax, Cavg, trough) across a range of doses for a structural PK model.

nodes.name: _interval_metrics
nodes.summary: Computes per-interval exposure metrics (AUC over tau, Cmax, Cavg, trough) from a simulated concentration profile.

nodes.name: dose_sweep
nodes.summary: Sweeps a model across multiple doses, simulating each to steady state and returning interval exposure metrics and the response envelope.

nodes.name: dosing.py
nodes.summary: Dosing-record utilities: expands NONMEM-style dose events (II/ADDL), detects multiple-dose regimens, and extracts steady-state dosing intervals from concentration records.

nodes.name: _dose_times
nodes.summary: Expands a dose row's interval (II) and additional-doses (ADDL) fields into explicit dose times.

nodes.name: is_multiple_dose
nodes.summary: Detects whether a record set represents a multiple-dose regimen.

nodes.name: extract_ss_intervals
nodes.summary: Extracts per-subject steady-state dosing intervals (time-after-dose profiles) from concentration records for steady-state NCA and fitting.

nodes.name: SSProfile
nodes.summary: Dataclass describing one subject's steady-state interval: time-after-dose, concentrations, dose, tau, number of doses, and trough source.

nodes.name: forecast.py
nodes.summary: Bayesian dose forecasting: MAP-estimates individual parameters from measured levels, simulates steady-state exposure, and optimizes the dose to hit a target metric.

nodes.name: _optimize_dose
nodes.summary: Optimizes the dose to achieve a target steady-state exposure metric for given individual parameters.

nodes.name: forecast
nodes.summary: End-to-end TDM forecast: derives individual PK via MAP from measured levels (or population typical), simulates, and optionally optimizes dose to target.

nodes.name: nca_ss.py
nodes.summary: Steady-state non-compartmental analysis computing per-subject AUC over tau, Cmax/Cmin, CL/F, and accumulation, then summarizing by dose.

nodes.name: nca_ss_subject
nodes.summary: Computes steady-state NCA parameters for one subject's dosing interval (AUCtau, Cmax, Cmin, Cavg, CL/F).

nodes.name: run_nca_ss
nodes.summary: Runs steady-state NCA over all subject profiles and summarizes results grouped by dose.

nodes.name: nca.py
nodes.summary: Single-dose non-compartmental analysis: lambda_z selection, AUC by trapezoid, Cmax/Tmax, CL/F, half-life, percent extrapolation, plus geometric-mean dose summaries.
nodes.languageNotes: Best lambda_z chosen by adjusted R^2 over terminal windows; supports manual refit.

nodes.name: _auc_intervals
nodes.summary: Computes interval AUCs by the linear trapezoidal rule over a concentration-time profile.

nodes.name: _best_lambda_z
nodes.summary: Selects the terminal elimination rate (lambda_z) by maximizing adjusted R^2 over candidate terminal windows.

nodes.name: refit_lambda_z_manual
nodes.summary: Refits lambda_z from a user-selected set of terminal points for manual NCA override.

nodes.name: nca_subject
nodes.summary: Computes full NCA parameters for one subject (Cmax, Tmax, AUClast, AUCinf, CL/F, Vz/F, t-half, % extrapolated) for IV or oral dosing.

nodes.name: summarize_by_dose
nodes.summary: Aggregates per-subject NCA parameters into geometric-mean and geometric-CV summaries grouped by dose.

nodes.name: run_nca
nodes.summary: Runs single-dose NCA across all subjects from records and dose mapping, returning per-subject and by-dose summaries.

nodes.name: nlme.py
nodes.summary: True nonlinear mixed-effects estimation for the PK model library: FOCE-I (Laplace conditional EBEs) and SAEM estimators sharing a lognormal IIV and residual-error model, plus stepwise covariate modeling, parameter uncertainty, and MAP estimation.
nodes.languageNotes: Laplace approximation with numeric Hessian for FOCE-I; stochastic-approximation EM for SAEM; SCM with warm-start and optional parallel batch fitting.

nodes.name: _build_cov_effects
nodes.summary: Builds covariate-effect descriptors from a model specification and subject covariates.

nodes.name: _ind_obj
nodes.summary: Individual conditional objective function (joint log-likelihood of data and random effects) minimized to find a subject's EBE.

nodes.name: _numeric_hessian
nodes.summary: Finite-difference numeric Hessian of an objective used for the Laplace approximation and uncertainty.

nodes.name: _conditional_mode
nodes.summary: Finds a subject's empirical Bayes estimate by minimizing the individual conditional objective.

nodes.name: _laplace_subject
nodes.summary: Laplace-approximated marginal likelihood contribution for one subject, integrating out random effects.

nodes.name: _population_ofv
nodes.summary: Population objective function value summed over all subjects' Laplace contributions.

nodes.name: _initial_theta
nodes.summary: Derives initial fixed-effect (theta) estimates from subject data before population fitting.

nodes.name: _parameter_uncertainty
nodes.summary: Estimates parameter standard errors and RSEs from the objective Hessian, with condition-number reporting.

nodes.name: _assemble
nodes.summary: Assembles the final NLME result object (theta, omega, sigma, OFV, EBEs, covariate records, uncertainty) from a completed fit.

nodes.name: focei_fit
nodes.summary: FOCE-I population fit: iteratively optimizes fixed effects and variance components using per-subject Laplace EBEs, returning estimates, shrinkage, and uncertainty.

nodes.name: _saem_estep
nodes.summary: SAEM expectation step: MCMC sampling of subject random effects given current parameters.

nodes.name: _saem_update_theta
nodes.summary: SAEM maximization step updating fixed effects and variance components from stochastic-approximation sufficient statistics.

nodes.name: saem_fit
nodes.summary: SAEM population fit: stochastic-approximation EM alternating MCMC E-step and parameter M-step, deterministic for a fixed seed.

nodes.name: population_fit
nodes.summary: Dispatches a population fit to FOCE-I or SAEM by method and returns the unified result.

nodes.name: _warm_init
nodes.summary: Warm-starts a candidate covariate fit from a parent result's estimates to accelerate SCM.

nodes.name: _fit_batch
nodes.summary: Fits a batch of candidate models, optionally across a process pool, for stepwise covariate modeling.

nodes.name: scm
nodes.summary: Stepwise covariate modeling: forward-selection then backward-elimination of covariate effects by OFV change, with warm-start and optional parallelism.

nodes.name: map_estimate
nodes.summary: Maximum a posteriori estimation of an individual's random effects from observed concentrations and a population prior (used by TDM forecasting).

nodes.name: _CovEffect
nodes.summary: Covariate-effect model: holds parameter/covariate linkage, centering, and a multiplier factor with describe/key helpers.

nodes.name: _PopSpec
nodes.summary: Population-model specification bundling structural model, IIV parameters, error model, and covariate effects for the estimators.

nodes.name: _Subject
nodes.summary: Internal per-subject record holding doses, observation times/concentrations, weight, and covariates for population fitting.

nodes.name: nmexport.py
nodes.summary: Generates NONMEM control streams and mrgsolve C++ model code from a fitted NLME result, mapping structural models to ADVAN/TRANS and translating IIV, residual error, and covariate effects.
nodes.languageNotes: Maps oral/IV 1-2 cmt fits to ADVAN2/TRANS2 and ADVAN3/TRANS4; covariate effects written as power-of-covariate THETA terms; ALAG1 for absorption lag.

nodes.name: build_nonmem
nodes.summary: Builds a NONMEM control stream from an NLME fit, selecting the ADVAN/TRANS subroutine and emitting $PK/$THETA/$OMEGA/$SIGMA with covariate and lag terms.

nodes.name: build_mrgsolve
nodes.summary: Builds an mrgsolve C++ model (ODE plus parameter/IIV/error blocks) from an NLME fit.

nodes.name: pk_fit.py
nodes.summary: Individual (naive-pooled / two-stage) fitting of structural PK and PK-PD models by nonlinear least squares, with Jacobian-based RSEs, dataset-level fitting, and AIC model comparison.

nodes.name: rse_from_jacobian
nodes.summary: Derives parameter relative standard errors from the least-squares Jacobian and residual sum of squares.

nodes.name: fit_subject_model
nodes.summary: Fits one subject's concentration data to a structural PK model by nonlinear least squares, returning parameters, RSEs, and fit metrics.

nodes.name: _pkpd_init
nodes.summary: Builds initial parameter guesses for a joint PK-PD fit from PK and PD observations.

nodes.name: fit_subject_pkpd
nodes.summary: Fits a subject's joint PK-PD model (concentration and effect endpoints) by nonlinear least squares.

nodes.name: fit_pk_dataset
nodes.summary: Fits a structural model to every subject in a dataset and summarizes parameters via two-stage statistics.

nodes.name: compare_models
nodes.summary: Ranks candidate structural models by AIC across the subject set.

nodes.name: pk_models.py
nodes.summary: Structural PK / PK-PD model library (18 models ported from the PopPK Workbench): each model is a pure-Python ODE system with allometric scaling, dosing-compartment conventions, and a concentration map, exposed through a registry.
nodes.languageNotes: Allometric exponents 0.75 for CL/Q/Vmax and 1.0 for volumes centered at 70 kg; includes Michaelis-Menten, transit-absorption, effect-compartment, and indirect-response (IDR) models.

nodes.name: list_models
nodes.summary: Returns the registry of available structural PK/PK-PD models.

nodes.name: get_model
nodes.summary: Looks up a structural model definition by its key.

nodes.name: PKModel
nodes.summary: Dataclass describing one structural model: key, label, group, IV/PD flags, parameters, defaults, compartment layout, ODE right-hand side, concentration map, init state, effect function, and lag parameter.

nodes.name: pk_simulate.py
nodes.summary: Numerical simulator integrating the structural PK/PK-PD ODE models over dose events and observation times, with allometric parameter scaling and a steady-state time-course helper.

nodes.name: scale_params
nodes.summary: Applies allometric weight scaling to a model's parameters before simulation.

nodes.name: simulate
nodes.summary: Integrates a structural model's ODE system across dose events and returns predicted concentrations at the requested observation times.

nodes.name: simulate_timecourse
nodes.summary: Simulates a dense steady-state concentration time-course for a multiple-dose regimen.

nodes.name: poppk.py
nodes.summary: Two-stage population summary statistics: geometric-mean typical values and between-subject IIV from individual fits, plus log-linear covariate-effect estimation.

nodes.name: two_stage_summary
nodes.summary: Summarizes individual-fit parameters into geometric-mean typical values and IIV (geometric CV) per parameter.

nodes.name: covariate_effect
nodes.summary: Estimates a log-linear covariate effect on a parameter from matched individual-fit and covariate values.

nodes.name: vpc.py
nodes.summary: Pure, deterministic observed-vs-predicted goodness-of-fit and visual predictive check (VPC, including prediction-corrected pcVPC) built on the structural PK/PK-PD simulator.
nodes.languageNotes: Simulation-based percentile bands under IIV with a fixed seed for reproducibility; pcVPC normalizes observations and simulations by typical prediction.

nodes.name: obs_vs_pred
nodes.summary: Pairs every positive observation with its individual prediction (IPRED) for goodness-of-fit plots and metrics.

nodes.name: _gof_log
nodes.summary: Computes log-scale goodness-of-fit metrics between observed and individually predicted concentrations.

nodes.name: vpc_band
nodes.summary: Builds simulation-based VPC percentile bands by sampling the population IIV model on a time grid with a fixed seed.

nodes.name: pcvpc
nodes.summary: Computes a prediction-corrected VPC: bins observations and simulations and normalizes by typical prediction to remove dosing/covariate heterogeneity.

nodes.name: store.py
nodes.summary: SQLite persistence layer for sessions and their audit trails: SessionStore saves and loads session state plus serialized audit chains keyed by session id.
nodes.languageNotes: Repository pattern over raw sqlite3; audit chain stored as serialized JSON alongside session state.

nodes.name: SessionStore
nodes.summary: SQLite repository persisting session records (owner, state, audit list) with save, load_all, and get accessors used by the orchestrator on startup and after each mutation.

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Empty package marker for the app.db package.

nodes.name: main.py
nodes.summary: FastAPI application entry point wiring all HTTP routes for PharmAgent: sessions, dataset upload, chat, workflows, every PK/NCA/BE/NLME/SCM analysis, async jobs, exports (CSV/control/CDISC), skills, adversarial review, reports, and the SPA frontend mount, with bearer auth and a request-context/error envelope.
nodes.languageNotes: FastAPI with pydantic request models per endpoint, dependency-injected ownership checks (owned_session/current_owner), and a request_context middleware adding request ids and a uniform error body.

nodes.name: request_context
nodes.summary: ASGI middleware that attaches a request id, logs structured request/response context, and normalizes errors into the standard envelope.

nodes.name: current_owner
nodes.summary: Auth dependency that resolves the calling owner from the bearer Authorization header, enforcing the configured API token.

nodes.name: upload_for_session
nodes.summary: Endpoint that accepts a dataset file upload for a session, persists it within the allowed data directory, and registers it on the session state.

nodes.name: run_nlme
nodes.summary: Endpoint that submits a population NLME fit as a background job and returns its job id for polling.

nodes.name: export_cdisc
nodes.summary: Endpoint that builds and streams the CDISC ADaM submission zip (ADPC/ADPP/define.xml) for a session.

nodes.name: run_skill
nodes.summary: Endpoint that replays a previously captured skill workflow against a session for the calling owner.

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Empty package marker for the app.tools package containing all agent tool modules.

nodes.name: base.py
nodes.summary: Tool framework foundation: ToolContext and ToolResult value objects, the Tool descriptor (with to_anthropic schema export), and a ToolRegistry that registers, looks up, scopes per-agent, and executes tools.
nodes.languageNotes: Registry + descriptor pattern; to_anthropic adapts each tool into the Anthropic tool-use schema consumed by RealLLM.

nodes.name: Tool
nodes.summary: Descriptor for a single callable tool (name, description, input schema, handler, agent scope) with to_anthropic to expose it to the LLM.

nodes.name: ToolRegistry
nodes.summary: Central registry of all tools supporting register, get, names, per-agent filtering (for_agent), and execute, which invokes a tool handler against a PharmState/context.

nodes.name: be_tools.py
nodes.summary: Bioequivalence Agent tools that derive test/reference geometric mean ratios and 90% confidence intervals from NCA exposures, exposed as the module's TOOLS for the registry.
nodes.languageNotes: Domain BE workflow: log-transformed exposure comparison; helpers resolve treatment columns and pick reference/test arms.

nodes.name: run_bioequivalence
nodes.summary: Computes the test-vs-reference GMR and 90% CI for PK exposures across treatment arms and writes the bioequivalence result to PharmState.

nodes.name: builtins.py
nodes.summary: Assembles the default ToolRegistry by collecting the TOOLS lists from every tool module (data, NCA, BE, dose-proportionality, compartmental, popPK, PK model, QC, report, regulatory, review).

nodes.name: default_registry
nodes.summary: Builds and returns a ToolRegistry populated with all tools from the bundled tool modules; the single wiring point used by the orchestrator.

nodes.name: compartmental_tools.py
nodes.summary: Compartmental Agent tools that fit 1- and 2-compartment oral PK models per subject and write a compacted comparison to PharmState, exposed via the module TOOLS.

nodes.name: fit_compartmental_models
nodes.summary: Fits one- and two-compartment oral models per subject, compares them, and stores a compact per-model summary (optionally steady-state) in PharmState.

nodes.name: data_tools.py
nodes.summary: Data Manager tools to load, profile, validate (CDISC), and visualize PK datasets, with path-sandboxing and role detection; exposed as TOOLS for the registry.
nodes.languageNotes: _safe_path enforces the data-dir allowlist (path-traversal guard) before any file read.

nodes.name: load_dataset
nodes.summary: Loads a dataset from a sandbox-validated path into PharmState, recording detected column roles and metadata.

nodes.name: profile_pk_dataset
nodes.summary: Profiles a loaded PK dataset (subjects, records, time/concentration ranges, role coverage) and stores the profile summary on PharmState.

nodes.name: validate_cdisc
nodes.summary: Checks a dataset against CDISC-style structural expectations and records validation findings on PharmState.

nodes.name: generate_spaghetti_plot
nodes.summary: Generates a per-subject concentration-vs-time spaghetti plot for the loaded dataset and stores the rendered figure on PharmState.

nodes.name: dp_tools.py
nodes.summary: Dose-Proportionality Agent tool that assesses dose proportionality via the power model over NCA exposures, exposed as the module TOOLS.

nodes.name: run_dose_proportionality
nodes.summary: Fits the power model (log exposure vs log dose) across dose levels to assess dose proportionality and writes the result to PharmState.

nodes.name: nca_tools.py
nodes.summary: NCA Agent tool computing non-compartmental PK parameters (Cmax, Tmax, AUC, half-life, lambda-z, etc.) including steady-state handling, exposed as the module TOOLS.
nodes.languageNotes: compute_nca is the batch's largest single function (~154 lines), covering full NCA parameter derivation with lambda-z regression.

nodes.name: compute_nca
nodes.summary: Computes the full non-compartmental parameter set per subject (exposure, peak, terminal-slope, half-life and steady-state metrics) and stores the NCA summary on PharmState.

nodes.name: pkmodel_tools.py
nodes.summary: Modeler Agent tool suite: fits/compares the structural PK model library, simulates profiles, runs VPC, true NLME, stepwise covariate modeling (SCM), MAP/TDM forecasting, diagnostics, and dose sweeps over PharmState.
nodes.languageNotes: Largest file in the batch (660 lines); _build_subjects assembles per-subject estimation data (with optional BLQ/M3 censoring) shared by fit/VPC/NLME/SCM/forecast.

nodes.name: _build_subjects
nodes.summary: Transforms the source dataframe into per-subject dosing/observation records (optionally flagging BLQ) used as the common input for all PK model fitting routines.

nodes.name: fit_pk_model
nodes.summary: Fits and compares candidate structural PK models from the library, selecting a best fit and storing parameters, fit metrics, and RSEs on PharmState.

nodes.name: simulate_pk_profile
nodes.summary: Simulates concentration-time profiles for a chosen model and dosing regimen and stores the simulated curves on PharmState.

nodes.name: run_vpc
nodes.summary: Runs a (prediction-corrected) visual predictive check by simulating replicate datasets and summarizing observed-vs-predicted percentiles into PharmState.

nodes.name: run_nlme
nodes.summary: Fits a true nonlinear mixed-effects population model (FOCE-I/SAEM) over the assembled subjects and stores thetas, omega/shrinkage, and sigma on PharmState.

nodes.name: run_scm
nodes.summary: Performs stepwise covariate modeling over candidate covariate-parameter relationships and records the selected covariate model on PharmState.

nodes.name: forecast_map
nodes.summary: Produces a MAP/TDM Bayesian individual forecast from prior population parameters and observed levels, storing the forecast on PharmState.

nodes.name: run_diagnostics
nodes.summary: Computes residual/goodness-of-fit diagnostics for the last fitted model and stores the diagnostic summary on PharmState.

nodes.name: run_dose_sweep
nodes.summary: Sweeps a range of doses through the fitted model to project exposure metrics and stores the dose-sweep table on PharmState.

nodes.name: config.py
nodes.summary: Application configuration via a pydantic Settings model: Anthropic API key/model, mock-LLM toggle, data and DB paths, auth token, CORS origins, and org branding. Centralizes runtime env wiring for the backend.
nodes.languageNotes: pydantic-settings BaseSettings pattern; allowed_data_dirs computes a path-traversal allowlist (sample_data + configured data_dir).

nodes.name: Settings
nodes.summary: pydantic settings object holding all backend runtime config; llm_is_mock decides mock vs real LLM and allowed_data_dirs returns the directory allowlist used to sandbox dataset file access.

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Empty package marker for the app.core package, which holds the orchestrator, audit chain, persistence, and supporting services.

nodes.name: audit.py
nodes.summary: Tamper-evident hash-chain audit trail: each AuditEntry links to the previous via SHA-256 over its inputs/outputs, and AuditChain appends, verifies, and (de)serializes the chain for Part-11-style provenance.
nodes.languageNotes: Hash-chaining (entry_hash includes prev_hash) makes any retroactive edit detectable; dataclasses + asdict for serialization.

nodes.name: AuditEntry
nodes.summary: Single immutable audit record (agent, tool, action, input/output hashes, actor, reason); compute_hash derives the chained entry hash and to_dict serializes it.

nodes.name: AuditChain
nodes.summary: Ordered chain of AuditEntry records with append (links new entries to the head hash), verify (re-derives every hash to detect tampering), and to_list/from_list round-tripping.

nodes.name: cdisc.py
nodes.summary: Builds CDISC ADaM-style regulatory deliverables from PharmState: ADPC (concentration records), ADPP (NCA parameters), and a define.xml metadata document, zipped together by build_package.
nodes.languageNotes: ADaM domain conventions (ADPC/ADPP) and define.xml emitted as escaped XML; CSVs written via csv.DictWriter into an in-memory zip.

nodes.name: build_adpp
nodes.summary: Constructs ADPP (PK parameter) rows from the stored NCA summary, mapping internal parameter keys to CDISC PARAMCD/PARAM labels.

nodes.name: build_adpc
nodes.summary: Constructs ADPC (concentration) rows from the source dataframe using detected ID/time/DV roles, coercing numerics and skipping missing values.

nodes.name: build_define_xml
nodes.summary: Generates a define.xml metadata document describing each emitted dataset and its variables, with XML-escaped study and column metadata.

nodes.name: build_package
nodes.summary: Assembles the full CDISC submission package: builds ADPP/ADPC/define.xml and writes them as CSV/XML into a single in-memory zip archive.

nodes.name: exporters.py
nodes.summary: CSV exporters for every analysis result held in PharmState (NCA, BE, dose-proportionality, PK model, dose sweep, VPC, NLME) plus NONMEM/mrgsolve control-file exporters, with availability discovery helpers.
nodes.languageNotes: Registry-style dispatch: per-result-kind formatter functions selected by `kind` in export_csv/export_control.

nodes.name: _pk_model
nodes.summary: Flattens stored PK model-fit results (best model parameters, fit metrics, relative standard errors) into CSV rows.

nodes.name: _nlme
nodes.summary: Serializes NLME population-fit output (thetas with RSE, omega/shrinkage, sigma) into a tidy CSV parameter table.

nodes.name: available
nodes.summary: Inspects PharmState and returns the list of analysis-result kinds that currently have data available for CSV export.

nodes.name: export_csv
nodes.summary: Dispatches to the matching per-kind CSV formatter, raising ValueError for unknown export kinds.

nodes.name: export_control
nodes.summary: Produces NONMEM/mrgsolve control-file text for the requested kind from stored NLME results, validating availability before emitting.

nodes.name: jobs.py
nodes.summary: Thread-pool background job manager for long-running tools (NLME, SCM): submit returns a job id, work runs off-request, and get polls status with bounded retention of finished jobs.
nodes.languageNotes: ThreadPoolExecutor + threading.Lock; _prune_locked caps stored job history to bound memory.

nodes.name: JobManager
nodes.summary: Manages async submission and polling of background tool runs via a thread pool, recording start/finish timestamps, results, and errors under a lock.

nodes.name: llm.py
nodes.summary: LLM client abstraction with an LLM protocol, a keyless MockLLM (regex/heuristic routing for tests), a RealLLM backed by the Anthropic SDK, and a get_llm factory that picks mock vs real from settings.
nodes.languageNotes: Protocol-style interface with mock/real implementations; MockLLM keeps the system runnable without API keys.

nodes.name: MockLLM
nodes.summary: Deterministic keyless LLM stand-in that classifies intent and selects tools via regex and keyword heuristics, enabling tests and offline runs.

nodes.name: RealLLM
nodes.summary: Anthropic-backed LLM client that performs intent classification and tool selection through the messages API using each tool's to_anthropic schema.

nodes.name: logging_config.py
nodes.summary: Structured JSON logging setup with a dependency-free JsonFormatter emitting one JSON line per record, plus configure_logging to install it from a level or env var.

nodes.name: JsonFormatter
nodes.summary: logging.Formatter that renders each record as a single JSON object including timestamp, level, message, extra fields, and formatted exceptions.

nodes.name: orchestrator.py
nodes.summary: Central orchestrator: owns sessions, routes chat to agents, executes PK/tool runs with per-session locking, runs the adversarial review loop and workflows, captures/replays skills, and persists state plus the audit chain.
nodes.languageNotes: Per-session RLock for thread safety; wires LLM, ToolRegistry, SessionStore, SkillStore, provenance, and workflows; every mutating op re-verifies the audit chain.

nodes.name: Session
nodes.summary: Dataclass bundling a session's PharmState, tool context, audit chain, chat history, pending review, params, owner, and command log.

nodes.name: Orchestrator
nodes.summary: Coordinates the whole backend: session lifecycle, ownership checks, chat routing, tool/PK execution, review loops, skill capture/replay, role overrides, and multi-step workflow advancement with persistence and audit verification.

nodes.name: pharmstate.py
nodes.summary: Defines PharmState, the typed pydantic communication bus shared across agents (datasets, roles, and every analysis result), plus apply_writes which validates and merges agent-produced field writes.
nodes.languageNotes: Single typed state object as an inter-agent contract; apply_writes enforces that only declared fields can be mutated.

nodes.name: PharmState
nodes.summary: Central typed pydantic model carrying dataset metadata, column roles, and all analysis outputs (NCA, BE, PK model, NLME, VPC, etc.) passed between agents.

nodes.name: apply_writes
nodes.summary: Validates and applies a set of agent-produced field writes onto a PharmState, rejecting writes to undeclared fields to keep the shared bus type-safe.

nodes.name: provenance.py
nodes.summary: Captures run provenance for reproducibility: key package versions, platform info, git SHA, app version, and SHA-256 content hashes of input files.
nodes.languageNotes: Best-effort version/git-SHA lookups degrade gracefully when metadata or git is unavailable.

nodes.name: collect_provenance
nodes.summary: Assembles a provenance record combining app version, dependency versions, platform details, and the current git commit SHA.

nodes.name: file_sha256
nodes.summary: Computes the SHA-256 hash of a file's contents in chunks for dataset content fingerprinting.

nodes.name: schema_extractor.py
nodes.summary: Privacy boundary that turns a raw dataframe into a non-identifying schema: detects PK column roles and emits per-column type and numeric-summary metadata without leaking row-level data.
nodes.languageNotes: Acts as a privacy gate — only aggregate summaries (not raw records) cross into LLM-visible state.

nodes.name: detect_roles
nodes.summary: Heuristically maps dataframe columns to PK roles (subject ID, time, concentration/DV, dose, etc.) from their names.

nodes.name: extract_schema
nodes.summary: Builds the privacy-safe dataset schema: per-column dtype, role assignment, and numeric summaries, packaged with a dataset id for downstream agents.

nodes.name: skills.py
nodes.summary: Captured, replayable analysis workflows: distill_steps condenses a session command log into reusable steps, the Skill model serializes to dict/markdown, and SkillStore persists skills in SQLite.
nodes.languageNotes: Skills are derived from the audit/command log, making analysis sequences reproducible and shareable.

nodes.name: distill_steps
nodes.summary: Condenses a session's raw command log into an ordered list of replayable skill steps, dropping non-reproducible noise.

nodes.name: Skill
nodes.summary: Represents a named, captured analysis workflow with ordered steps; serializes to dict and human-readable markdown.

nodes.name: SkillStore
nodes.summary: SQLite-backed repository for captured skills with save, get, list, and delete operations.

nodes.name: poppk_tools.py
nodes.summary: PopPK agent tool exposing a deterministic two-stage (STS) population summary and covariate screen, deriving typical values and IIV from individual compartmental or NCA fits rather than a true NLME fit.

nodes.name: run_poppk
nodes.summary: Tool entrypoint that builds per-subject parameter rows, computes two-stage typical value and geometric-CV IIV, and screens covariate relationships, returning a ToolResult summary.

nodes.name: _covariate_by_subject
nodes.summary: Maps a detected covariate column to a per-subject numeric value by grouping the dataset on the subject role and taking each subject's first usable covariate observation.

nodes.name: _from_compartmental
nodes.summary: Extracts per-subject CL/V/KA parameter rows from a previously stored compartmental fit in state for use as the two-stage input.

nodes.name: qc_tools.py
nodes.summary: Quality-control agent tool that runs a battery of pass/warn/fail checks over dataset and analysis state (record counts, data quality, parameter plausibility) and aggregates an overall QC verdict.

nodes.name: run_qc
nodes.summary: Runs the full QC checklist against pharm state, appending individual check results and issues, then returns a ToolResult with counts of passed, warned, and failed checks.

nodes.name: regulatory_tools.py
nodes.summary: Generates an ICH/FDA Module 2.7.2 (Summary of Clinical Pharmacology) Word document from analysis state, assembling narrative sections (background, NCA, popPK, special populations, DDI, conclusions) and standardized tables.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses python-docx (Document, Pt) to build a structured regulatory submission document with headings, tables, and page breaks.

nodes.name: generate_272
nodes.summary: Tool entrypoint that assembles the full 2.7.2 document — title page, provenance, all narrative sections and summary tables — saves it to disk, and returns a ToolResult with the report path and study info.

nodes.name: _section_24_special_pops
nodes.summary: Builds the special-populations section by classifying selected covariates (renal, hepatic, age, weight, sex, other) into subsections and rendering each covariate effect narrative.

nodes.name: _section_26_conclusions
nodes.summary: Synthesizes a conclusions section, narrating the population model, IIV, selected covariates, and key exposure findings drawn from analysis state.

nodes.name: _section_22_poppk
nodes.summary: Renders the population-PK narrative subsection covering estimation method, structural model, parameter estimates, IIV, residual error, and VPC/GOF outcomes.

nodes.name: _section_23_analyses
nodes.summary: Renders the additional-analyses section, summarizing dose-proportionality and bioequivalence results when present in state.

nodes.name: _table_2722_poppk
nodes.summary: Builds Table 2.7.2.2 of population-PK parameter estimates with RSE, IIV omega, shrinkage, and residual sigma rows.

nodes.name: _table_2723_covariates
nodes.summary: Builds Table 2.7.2.3 listing selected covariate effects (coefficient, exponent, ΔOFV) and standalone covariate-effect screens.

nodes.name: _table_2721_nca
nodes.summary: Builds Table 2.7.2.1 of NCA exposure parameters (Cmax, Tmax, AUClast, AUCinf, t1/2, CL/F) grouped by dose.

nodes.name: _section_21_background
nodes.summary: Renders the background and overview section of the 2.7.2 document from study metadata.

nodes.name: _section_22_nca
nodes.summary: Renders the non-compartmental analysis narrative subsection describing exposure metrics and methodology.

nodes.name: _kv_table
nodes.summary: Shared helper that renders a headed key-value table into the document, or a placeholder line when there are no rows.

nodes.name: report_tools.py
nodes.summary: Generates a general analysis report Word document from state, rendering per-subject and dose-summary NCA tables, extra sections for BE and dose-proportionality, and a reproducibility/provenance section.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses python-docx to build the report with tables and a deferred import of collect_provenance for the reproducibility section.

nodes.name: generate_report
nodes.summary: Tool entrypoint that builds the analysis report document — NCA summary/subject tables, extra analysis sections, and reproducibility — saves it, and returns a ToolResult with the report path.

nodes.name: _extra_sections
nodes.summary: Appends optional report sections for bioequivalence and dose-proportionality results when those analyses are present in state.

nodes.name: _reproducibility_section
nodes.summary: Renders a reproducibility section capturing package versions, git SHA, and dataset hash via collect_provenance for audit traceability.

nodes.name: _summary_table
nodes.summary: Renders the dose-grouped NCA summary statistics table into the report document.

nodes.name: _subject_table
nodes.summary: Renders the per-subject NCA parameter table, sorted by a stable subject key.

nodes.name: _dose_grouped_meaningfully
nodes.summary: Heuristic that decides whether the NCA summary contains more than one meaningful dose group, controlling whether a dose-grouped table is shown.

nodes.name: review_tools.py
nodes.summary: Reviewer agent tool that wraps the adversarial compute module, running independent recomputation and consistency checks over analysis results to surface findings.

nodes.name: adversarial_review
nodes.summary: Tool entrypoint that runs the adversarial review over current state and raw data, returning findings and an overall goal-met verdict as a ToolResult.

nodes.name: workflows.py
nodes.summary: Defines ordered workflow templates (currently the end-to-end nca_full pipeline) mapping steps to agent/tool assignments with human-review gates, plus a get_workflow lookup.

nodes.name: conftest.py
nodes.summary: Pytest bootstrap that inserts the backend root onto sys.path so the app package is importable during test collection.

nodes.name: pharmagent.db
nodes.summary: SQLite database file backing session persistence and audit storage for the PharmAgent backend.

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Empty package marker making the tests directory an importable Python package.

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Empty package marker making the reference-validation tests an importable Python package.

nodes.name: README.md
nodes.summary: Explains the reference-validation suite: how it drives the shipping compute path on canonical public datasets (Theophylline) to assert literature concordance, cross-method consistency, and optional exact tool concordance.

nodes.name: references.py
nodes.summary: Pins canonical public datasets (Theophylline) to published popPK literature consensus values with acceptance bands, plus an optional slot for the user's own NONMEM/Monolix/nlmixr2 run for exact concordance.

nodes.name: test_theophylline.py
nodes.summary: Reference-validation test that drives the shipping NCA + FOCE-I + SAEM path on the Theophylline dataset and asserts estimates fall within published literature bands and agree across methods.

nodes.name: test_adversarial.py
nodes.summary: Tests the adversarial review compute and its HTTP endpoint: that clean state meets the goal, that data inconsistencies are flagged at correct severities, recomputation triggers, and the review loop terminates.

nodes.name: test_api.py
nodes.summary: FastAPI endpoint tests covering public health/models routes, the session-and-NCA workflow, auth enforcement, CSV upload validation, and background-job submission/polling.

nodes.name: test_audit_and_state.py
nodes.summary: Unit tests verifying the audit hash-chain stays intact and detects tampering, and that PharmState enforces per-field ownership.

nodes.name: test_be_compute.py
nodes.summary: Unit tests for the bioequivalence compute, checking GMR and confidence intervals against hand-computed values for paired and parallel designs and the overall BE verdict logic.

nodes.name: test_cdisc.py
nodes.summary: Tests CDISC ADaM export: that ADPP is built in long BDS form with standard PARAMCDs and that the package zip contains valid define.xml, including the no-dataframe path.

nodes.name: test_compartmental_compute.py
nodes.summary: Unit tests for compartmental models: closed-form agreement and vectorization of 1-/2-cmt oral concentration, parameter recovery from a simulated fit, and grouped model selection.

nodes.name: test_diagnostics.py
nodes.summary: Unit tests for residual diagnostics and NPDE: identity fits give zero IWRES, PRED uses typical not individual parameters, and NPDE is well-calibrated, bounded, and deterministic for a fixed seed.

nodes.name: test_dose_sweep.py
nodes.summary: Unit tests for the dose-sweep compute: linear PK is dose-proportional, Michaelis-Menten is super-proportional, output envelope/metadata shapes are consistent, and Cavg equals AUC over tau.

nodes.name: test_dp_compute.py
nodes.summary: Unit tests for dose-proportionality power-model estimation: slope recovery for proportional/sub-/super-proportional data, closed-form critical region and CI, and aggregation across parameters.

nodes.name: test_forecast.py
nodes.summary: Unit tests for MAP/TDM forecasting: Bayesian update pulls individual estimates toward truth, no-levels falls back to population typical, dose optimization hits a target, and steady-state metrics are ordered.

nodes.name: test_identity_audit.py
nodes.summary: Tests identity-aware tamper-evident auditing: actor/reason recording with chain verification, tampering detection, backward compatibility, provenance stamping, and signed human-review entries.

nodes.name: test_nca_compute.py
nodes.summary: Unit tests for non-compartmental analysis: exact lambda_z and t1/2, AUCinf against analytic truth, Cmax/Tmax, CL/V, small percent-extrapolation, and dose grouping.

nodes.name: test_nlme.py
nodes.summary: Comprehensive unit tests for the NLME estimation engine: simulates synthetic populations with known parameters and verifies FOCE-I and SAEM recover theta/omega/sigma, plus covariate models, SCM selection, parameter uncertainty (RSE), and M3 BLQ censored-likelihood handling.
nodes.languageNotes: Heavy use of pytest fixtures (session-scoped synthetic populations) and pytest.approx tolerance-based parameter-recovery assertions; numpy RNG seeding makes simulations deterministic.

nodes.name: test_nmexport.py
nodes.summary: Unit tests for NONMEM control-stream and mrgsolve C++ code generation, asserting correct ADVAN/TRANS selection, power-model covariate THETA blocks, ALAG lag-time handling on depot, and None return for unsupported models.

nodes.name: test_orchestrator.py
nodes.summary: Integration tests for the agent Orchestrator and Supervisor: verifies LLM-based routing, workflow pause at the human-review gate, resume-to-report completion, privacy (metadata-only), SQLite persistence across restart, and session ownership enforcement.

nodes.name: test_pk_models.py
nodes.summary: Unit tests for the 18-model PK/PKPD library: validates analytic closed-form agreement (IV/oral 1-2cmt), allometric scaling, Michaelis-Menten saturability, transit-compartment peak delay, Emax/IDR PKPD behavior, parameter fitting, and AIC-based model comparison.

nodes.name: test_poppk_compute.py
nodes.summary: Unit tests for two-stage popPK summary statistics: closed-form verification of geometric mean, geometric CV%, typical-value/IIV summarization, and log-linear covariate-effect estimation with edge cases for nonpositive and insufficient data.

nodes.name: test_report_272.py
nodes.summary: Tests for the FDA Section 2.7.2 regulatory report generator: validates placeholder handling for empty state, NCA/NLME/SCM table population, study-info propagation, reproducibility section, and HTTP endpoints for report generation and download.

nodes.name: test_skills.py
nodes.summary: Tests for the skill capture/replay subsystem: verifies step distillation (dropping I/O and profiling, collapsing duplicates), SkillStore save/get/list/delete with versioning, markdown rendering, and end-to-end replay reproducing an NCA workflow on a fresh session, including HTTP routes.

nodes.name: test_ss_compute.py
nodes.summary: Unit tests for steady-state PK computation: confirms SS concentration formulas match single-dose superposition and reduce correctly at large tau, SS fitting recovers parameters, multiple-dose detection, and steady-state NCA grouping by dose.

nodes.name: test_vpc.py
nodes.summary: Unit tests for VPC and goodness-of-fit diagnostics: validates CV-to-SD conversion, observed-vs-predicted GOF metrics, simulation-based VPC band ordering and lognormal spread, determinism under fixed seed, and prediction-corrected VPC (pcVPC) structure and coverage.

nodes.name: _make_population
nodes.summary: Simulates a synthetic NLME population with known CL/V/KA typical values and lognormal IIV plus residual error, returning subject records used as ground truth for parameter-recovery assertions.

nodes.name: _make_cov_population
nodes.summary: Builds a synthetic population whose clearance depends on a weight covariate with a known exponent, used to test covariate-model recovery and stepwise covariate modeling selection.

nodes.name: _censored_population
nodes.summary: Generates a population with concentrations censored below an LLOQ and a parallel BLQ flag array, used to test the M3 censored-likelihood estimation path.

nodes.name: test_sparse_subject_is_handled
nodes.summary: Verifies FOCE-I fitting tolerates a subject with very few observations without crashing and still returns finite estimates for the full cohort.

nodes.name: test_scm_selects_true_covariate_and_rejects_noise
nodes.summary: Runs stepwise covariate modeling on a synthetic population and asserts the true weight-on-CL covariate is selected while a noise covariate is rejected.

nodes.name: _fit
nodes.summary: Helper that constructs a minimal fitted-model dict (model key, theta, IIV, covariate spec) used as input to the NONMEM and mrgsolve code builders under test.

nodes.name: test_persistence_survives_restart
nodes.summary: Creates a session and workflow under one Orchestrator backed by an on-disk SessionStore, then reconstructs a fresh Orchestrator and asserts the session and intact audit chain are recovered.

nodes.name: test_pkpd_dual_endpoint_fit_recovers
nodes.summary: Simulates a coupled PK/PD dual-endpoint dataset and verifies the joint fitter recovers the known PK and PD parameters within tolerance.

nodes.name: test_simulate_timecourse_single_and_multidose
nodes.summary: Checks the timecourse simulator produces correct single-dose and multiple-dose accumulation profiles across the model library.

nodes.name: test_two_stage_summary_typical_value_and_iiv
nodes.summary: Verifies the two-stage summary computes the correct geometric-mean typical value and inter-individual variability from per-subject parameter estimates.

nodes.name: test_covariate_effect_perfect_log_linear
nodes.summary: Constructs a perfect log-linear covariate relationship and asserts the covariate-effect estimator recovers the exact slope.

nodes.name: _nlme_state
nodes.summary: Builds a synthetic PharmState populated with NLME results, used to test that the Section 2.7.2 report renders model tables and conclusions.

nodes.name: test_full_state_no_crash
nodes.summary: Smoke test asserting the 2.7.2 report generator runs end-to-end without error on a fully populated state combining NCA, NLME, and SCM results.

nodes.name: test_http_272_download
nodes.summary: Exercises the HTTP endpoint that downloads a generated 2.7.2 report file via the FastAPI TestClient.

nodes.name: test_http_capture_list_get_run_delete
nodes.summary: End-to-end HTTP test covering the full skill lifecycle: capture from a session, list, get, run (replay), and delete via the FastAPI TestClient.

nodes.name: test_capture_then_inspect_steps
nodes.summary: Captures a skill from an executed NCA session and asserts the distilled steps reflect the tool calls performed.

nodes.name: test_extract_multiple_dose
nodes.summary: Verifies steady-state dosing-interval extraction correctly identifies multiple-dose regimens and partitions the profile into per-tau intervals.

nodes.name: _make_subject
nodes.summary: Helper that builds a single subject record with given parameters and weight covariate for use across the VPC and GOF tests.

nodes.name: _correct_model_population
nodes.summary: Simulates a population from the correct model so that pcVPC band coverage of the observed data can be validated.

nodes.name: test_obs_vs_pred_identity_is_perfect_fit
nodes.summary: Asserts that when individual predictions equal observations, the observed-vs-predicted GOF metrics report a perfect fit.

nodes.name: test_vpc_band_lognormal_spread_matches_cv
nodes.summary: Verifies the simulated VPC band spread matches the lognormal IIV implied by the specified coefficient of variation.

layers.name: API & Entry
layers.description: FastAPI application bootstrap and HTTP surface: app entrypoint (main.py), runtime settings (config.py), top-level workflow wiring (workflows.py), and DB package init that mount the agentic pharmacometrics service.

layers.name: Agent Layer
layers.description: LLM agent orchestration: the supervisor that drives multi-step pharmacometrics reasoning, the base agent abstraction, and the agent/tool definitions registry.

layers.name: Tool Layer
layers.description: Agent-callable tool implementations (PK model, NCA, BE, dose-proportionality, compartmental, poppk, report, regulatory, QC, review, data tools) plus the tool base/registry that bridge agents to the deterministic compute engine.

layers.name: Compute Engine
layers.description: Deterministic PK/PKPD numerical core: structural PK models, simulation/forecast, NCA (incl. steady-state), NLME (FOCE-I/SAEM/poppk), bioequivalence, dose-proportionality, dose-sweep, VPC, diagnostics, adversarial checks, and NONMEM export.

layers.name: Core Orchestration & Infrastructure
layers.description: Cross-cutting runtime services: the orchestrator, SHA-256 audit chain, background job runner, persistence store, LLM client, pharm-state, provenance/reproducibility, CDISC/exporters, schema extraction, skills, and logging config.

layers.name: Test Suite
layers.description: Pytest suite covering compute modules, API, orchestrator, audit/state, NLME, VPC, and the published-literature reference-validation tests (Theophylline) plus shared fixtures.

layers.name: Infrastructure
layers.description: Containerization and deployment: the multi-stage Dockerfile (builder and runtime stages) and the .dockerignore that package the backend for uvicorn-based serving.

layers.name: Project Config & Docs
layers.description: Project-level configuration (.env.example, ruff.toml, understand-anything configs/scan), dependency manifests (requirements*.txt), reference-test README, and the SQLite database artifact supporting the build and tooling environment.
